notes:
“Thay vì” means “instead of” — you use it when choosing one option and not another. It shows replacement.

It usually comes before a verb or noun phrase.  

After thay vì, the verb doesn’t change form — just the base verb. The main action (the real choice) comes in the other part of the sentence.

Close idea: “chứ không phải” (but not) — stronger contrast, often for correcting something, not just choosing.

translation.en: I eat rice instead of bread.

example.en: I will walk instead of taking the bus.
example.es: Caminaré en lugar de tomar el autobús.
example.vi: Tôi sẽ đi bộ thay vì đi xe buýt.

definitions.es: en lugar de; en vez de

exampleSentencesTranslated: He decided to walk to school instead of riding a motorbike.
She drinks plain water instead of drinking soft drinks every day.
You should do your homework right away instead of watching movies all night.
notes_md:
**“Thay vì”** means **“instead of”** — you use it when choosing one option and not another. It shows replacement.

It usually comes before a verb or noun phrase.  

After *thay vì*, the verb doesn’t change form — just the base verb. The main action (the real choice) comes in the other part of the sentence.

Close idea: **“chứ không phải”** (but not) — stronger contrast, often for correcting something, not just choosing.

translation.en: I will walk instead of driving.

content.sentence: Tôi sẽ đi bộ ___ lái xe.

exampleSentences: Anh quyết định đi bộ đến trường thay vì đi xe máy.
Chị uống nước lọc thay vì uống nước ngọt mỗi ngày.
Em nên làm bài tập ngay thay vì xem phim cả tối.
